name: recon-sploit
summary:
recon-sploit
Tool to list vulnerabilities that may be exploitable without access to the target (with shodan)

documentation.readme: recon-sploit Tool to list vulnerabilities that may be exploitable without access to the target (with shodan)
usage_sections: Features: Get version information and vulnerability list from Shodan and Censys Search for exploit in Exploit-DB and Vulners
Usage: If you want to use censys, rename .env.sample to .env and fill in your credentials.

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.name: --help
arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

examples: usage: recon-sploit.py [-h] (-l DOMAIN_LIST | -d DOMAIN | --cpe CPE | --cve CVE) [--show-duplicate SHOW_DUPLICATE]

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with thewhiteh4t/seeker (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with iojw/socialscan (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with devanshbatham/ParamSpider (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'recon' phase against surfaces [web] in the OSINT category. Fits when the engagement needs: recon-sploit
Tool to list vulnerabilities that may be exploitable without access to the target (with shodan).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: recon-sploit Tool to list vulnerabilities that may be exploitable without access to the target (with shodan).
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `usage: recon-sploit.py [-h] (-l DOMAIN_LIST | -d DOMAIN | --cpe CPE | --cve CVE) [--show-duplicate SHOW_DUPLICATE]`. Further invocations from upstream: `Run recon-sploit.py with arguments`; `python3 recon-sploit.py -d example.com`. recon-sploit Tool to list vulnerabilities that may be exploitable without access to the target (with shodan).
